action.content: Write a funny poem for kids about a product that you would like to sell. The poem should have exactly 6 stanzas. Separated the stanzas using 3 asterisk markers: ***
action.label: context

action.content:
Oh, I’m selling socks with tiny wings,  
They flutter off your toes like tiny springs!  
One minute they’re snug and neat and tight,  
Next minute they’re dancing in the night!  

***  

They wear sunglasses, they sing opera,  
They juggle marbles—look, they can’t stop ya!  
They’ll hop on your bed and sing a tune,  
Then vanish in a puff of fuzzy moon.  

***  

You’ll find them on your ceiling fan,  
Or hiding in your mom’s left shoe pan.  
They’ll tickle your toe when you’re asleep,  
Then steal your pillow while you’re deep in dream.  

***  

They’re not for walking, they’re for flying,  
They’ll zoom across the kitchen, sighing.  
“Why do you wear shoes?” they’ll ask with glee,  
“Let’s all float to the ceiling—free as can be!”  

***  

So buy a pair before they’re gone,  
Before they fly to Mars or the dawn.  
They’ll teach your cat how to juggle too,  
And turn your laundry into a zoo!  

***  

Now go, grab your sock-wing kit,  
And let your toes take flight—what a hit!  
Just don’t blame me if your socks all vanish…  
They’ve got a life of their own, and they’re not asking!
action.label: response
